title: 3D interpolation of irregular data with a POCS algorithm
authors: Abma, R., Kabir, N.
abstract: We present a Projection Onto Convex Sets algorithm for 3D interpolation of irregularly sampled seismic data. The method alternates between thresholding the F-K spectrum and re-imposing known traces, converging to a band-limited reconstruction without requiring user-tuned regularization weights.

title: Simultaneous seismic data denoising and reconstruction via multichannel singular spectrum analysis
authors: Oropeza, V., Sacchi, M.
abstract: Multichannel singular spectrum analysis (MSSA) reorganizes f-x slices into block Hankel matrices whose low-rank structure encodes coherent events. Truncated SVD then both denoises and reconstructs missing traces in one pass.

title: Simply denoise: wavefield reconstruction via jittered undersampling
authors: Hennenfent, G., Herrmann, F. J.
abstract: We exploit curvelet sparsity for both denoising and reconstruction of seismic wavefields. Combined with jittered undersampling, l1-promoted curvelet recovery outperforms regular subsampling and band-pass denoising.

title: Seismic data interpolation through convolutional U-Net
authors: Wang, B., Liu, N., Chen, Y.
abstract: We adapt the U-Net encoder-decoder for seismic 2D interpolation, training on synthetic gathers with random trace masks. Skip connections preserve high-frequency content, achieving SNR gains of 5–8 dB over POCS on the Mobil AVO benchmark.

title: Seismic data interpolation using generative adversarial networks
authors: Li, J., Chen, X., Wang, Y.
abstract: We propose a conditional GAN for seismic trace interpolation. The discriminator constrains the generator toward phase-consistent reconstructions, addressing the over-smoothing common in MSE-trained networks.

title: Random noise attenuation by a deep residual learning network
authors: Zhang, M., Lin, J.
abstract: We retrain a deep residual CNN (DnCNN) on synthetic Marmousi gathers contaminated with additive Gaussian noise. The network predicts the noise component, with batch normalization stabilizing training under varying input SNRs.

title: SeisFormer: a window-attention transformer for seismic processing
authors: Chen, K., Xu, P., Zhao, L.
abstract: SeisFormer adapts the Swin transformer with shifted window attention along the trace axis. By capturing long-range trace coherence missed by purely convolutional models, it sets new SOTA on the Mobil AVO Viking Graben benchmark.

title: Seismic denoising as a diffusion-based inverse problem
authors: Park, S., Kim, J., Lee, H.
abstract: We frame seismic denoising as posterior sampling under a score-based diffusion prior. A measurement-consistency term steers the reverse SDE, recovering low-amplitude reflections that discriminative networks miss at -5 dB input SNR.

title: First-arrival picking with a U-shaped convolutional network
authors: Hu, L., Zheng, X., Liu, Y.
abstract: PickNet is a 1D U-shaped CNN that predicts a Gaussian-shaped likelihood of first-break arrival per sample. It improves over STA/LTA by ~20 percentage points in accuracy at low SNR.

title: PhaseNet: a deep-neural-network-based seismic arrival-time picking method
authors: Zhu, W., Beroza, G. C.
abstract: PhaseNet is a U-Net trained on three-component waveforms to output per-sample P/S phase probabilities. While developed for earthquake catalogs, it transfers cleanly to active-source first-break picking.

title: Unrolled POCS networks for seismic interpolation
authors: Müller, A., Schmidt, R., Becker, T.
abstract: We unroll N iterations of POCS into a trainable network where each thresholding step is replaced with a learned proximal operator. The hybrid retains data-consistency guarantees while matching CNN reconstruction quality.

title: SeisBERT: a pre-trained foundation model for seismic data processing
authors: Xu, Z., Wang, J., Zhou, R.
abstract: SeisBERT pre-trains a masked-trace transformer on 50 TB of unlabeled marine and land surveys, then fine-tunes per task. A single backbone reaches near-SOTA on interpolation, denoising, and first-arrival picking with as few as 100 labeled examples.

title: Conditional Denoising Diffusion Probabilistic Model for Ground-Roll Attenuation
authors: Li, Y., Zhang, H., Huang, J., Li, Z.
abstract: Uses conditional denoising diffusion probabilistic model (c-DDPM) for seismic ground-roll attenuation; improved model generates clean data and ground roll simultaneously with better separation performance

title: Gabor-Based Learnable Sparse Representation for Self-Supervised Denoising
authors: Liu, S., Cheng, S., Alkhalifah, T. A.
org: King Abdullah University of Science and Technology
abstract: Proposes a Gabor-based learnable sparse representation network for self-supervised seismic denoising by constraining Gabor filter parameters

title: Ground Roll Attenuation in Seismic Data Based on Enhanced Deep Learning Framework With Adaptive Frequency Modulation Loss
authors: Sun, S., Li, H., Li, G., Wang, Z.
abstract: Proposes an enhanced U-Net with attention and residual modules, combined with MSE and adaptive frequency modulation loss for seismic ground roll attenuation

title: Near-Surface-Related Nonstationary Coherent Noise Suppression Using a Physically Constrained Deep Neural Network
authors: Liu, J., Hu, T., Wang, C., Li, X., Zeng, Q., Liu, L.
abstract: Proposes a physically constrained deep neural network with multi-modal physical constraints and SDPAF preprocessing to suppress nonstationary near-surface coherent noise

title: Unsupervised Deep Learning for Ground Roll and Scattered Noise Attenuation
authors: Liu, D., Sacchi, M. D., Wang, X., Chen, W.
abstract: Proposes an unsupervised deep learning method using U-Net generator with LMO and NMO corrections to extract self-similar features for ground roll and scattered noise attenuation

title: Ground-Roll Attenuation Using a Dual-Filter-Bank Convolutional Neural Network
authors: Zhang, C., van der Baan, M.
abstract: Proposes dual-filter-bank CNN with radial trace transform to separately extract broad- and narrow-scale features for ground-roll attenuation

title: Self-Supervised Ground-Roll Noise Attenuation Using Self-Labeling and Paired Data Synthesis
authors: Oliveira, D. A. B., Semin, D. G., Zaytsev, S.
abstract: Proposes a two-stage self-supervised framework using U-Net for ground-roll region detection and cGAN for noise attenuation via synthetic paired data

title: Deep learning with soft attention mechanism for small-scale ground roll attenuation
authors: Yang, L., Fomel, S., Wang, S., Chen, X., Chen, Y.
abstract: Proposes a supervised deep learning framework with soft attention residual learning for small-scale ground roll attenuation

title: Unsupervised ground-roll attenuation via implicit neural representations
authors: Li, J., Liu, D., Sacchi, M. D.
abstract: Proposes an unsupervised ground-roll attenuation method using implicit neural representations with NMO correction and horizontal derivative regularization

title: Signal separation using unsupervised learning with local uncorrelated constraints: Applications to ground-roll contaminated data sets
authors: Sun, X., Chai, X., Yang, Q., Zhai, M., Gu, H.
abstract: Proposes an unsupervised learning method with local uncorrelated constraints for ground-roll signal separation

title: Physics-constrained deep learning for ground roll attenuation
authors: Pham, N., Li, W.
abstract: Proposes a physics-constrained deep learning method with three CNN components for seismic ground roll attenuation

title: Ground-roll attenuation using generative adversarial networks
authors: Yuan, Y., Si, X., Zheng, Y.
abstract: Proposes a conditional GAN with U-Net generator for ground-roll attenuation, which effectively removes noise and preserves signals better than traditional methods

title: Improved Anomalous Amplitude Attenuation Method Based on Deep Neural Networks
authors: Tian, X., Lu, W., Li, Y.
abstract: Proposes improved anomalous amplitude attenuation (IAAA) method using FCN to detect noise regions and adaptively optimize AAA parameters, enhancing noise suppression and signal preservation

title: Seismic shear wave noise suppression and application to well tie
authors: Sun, H., Liu, E., Zhang, F., Wang, W., Li, X.
org: China University of Petroleum (Beijing) & Northeast Petroleum University
abstract: Proposes a shear-wave noise suppression workflow: filter linear/single-frequency noise, F-K filtering for far-offset multiples, MSI for near-offset multiples; improves SNR and well-tie accuracy

title: A reweighted damped singular spectrum analysis method for robust seismic noise suppression
authors: Huang, W.-L., Zhou, Y.-X., Zhou, Y., Liu, W.-J., Li, J.-D.
abstract: Proposes reweighted damped singular spectrum analysis (RD-SSA) for robust seismic incoherent/erratic noise suppression, combining damping operator and Tukey’s bisquare iterative reweighting

title: Seismic multiple suppression based on a deep neural network method for
authors: Wang, K., Hu, T., Wang, S., Wei, J.
org: Peking University, China University of Petroleum (Beijing)
abstract: Proposes a deep neural network (DNN) with data augmentation training for marine seismic multiple suppression and primary reconstruction by adding noise and rotation to enhance robustness

title: Surface-related multiple attenuation based on a self-supervised deep neural network with local wavefield characteristics
authors: Wang, K., Hu, T., Zhao, B., Wang, S.
org: Peking University, PetroChina, China University of Petroleum (Beijing)
abstract: Proposes a self-supervised deep neural network with multi-attention U-Net and local wavefield characteristic loss (MAE+LNCC) for surface-related multiple attenuation without labeled data

title: An in-depth study of U-net for seismic data conditioning: Multiple removal by moveout discrimination
authors: Durall, R., Ghanim, A., Ettrich, N., Keuper, J.
abstract: Proposes a U-Net model trained on synthetic data for postmigration multiple removal via moveout discrimination, achieving strong generalization and preserving data features without parameter tuning

title: Free-surface multiple attenuation and seismic deghosting for blended data
authors: Kiraz, M. S. R., Snieder, R., Sheiman, J.
org: Shell International, Colorado School of Mines
abstract: Proposes a 1D CNN for simultaneous free-surface multiple attenuation and seismic deghosting directly on blended data without prior deblending, robust to irregular acquisition and delay time variations

title: An Unsupervised Learning Method to Suppress Seismic Internal Multiples Based on Adaptive Virtual Events and Joint Constraints of Multiple Deep Neural Networks
authors: Wang, K., Hu, T., Zhao, B., Wang, S.
org: Peking University, PetroChina, China University of Petroleum
abstract: Proposes an unsupervised learning method (JCMDNNs) combining adaptive virtual events (AVE) and multi-DNN joint constraints (ensemble learning) for internal multiple suppression; no real labels needed, outperforms single DNN and L1-norm methods

title: Physics-Informed Self-Supervised Learning With Phase Resemblance Constraint for Internal Multiple Attenuation
authors: Liu, X., Hu, T., Wang, S.
org: Peking University, China University of Petroleum
abstract: Proposes physics-informed self-supervised learning (MPR-SSN) with phase resemblance (PR) constraint for internal multiple attenuation; uses U-Net+ECA+PDC, VE-predicted multiples as input, hybrid MAE+PR loss to avoid primary distortion, outperforms L1 and pure data-driven networks

title: Seismic Internal Multiple Attenuation Based on Unsupervised Deep Learning With a Local Orthogonalization Constraint
authors: Wang, K., Hu, T., Zhao, B.
abstract: Proposes unsupervised deep learning with local orthogonalization constraint (UDL-LOC) for internal multiple attenuation; uses multi-attention U-Net (MA-net) and AVE-predicted multiples as input, hybrid MAE+LOC loss to avoid primary leakage, outperforms UDL-CVE in synthetic and land field data

title: Unsupervised Learning for Seismic Internal Multiple Suppression Based on Adaptive Virtual Events
authors: Wang, K., Hu, T., Wang, S.
org: Peking University, China University of Petroleum
abstract: Proposes unsupervised deep learning combined with adaptive virtual events (AVE) for seismic internal multiple suppression; uses U-Net-like DNN as nonlinear matching operator, no true primary labels needed, outperforms traditional virtual event methods in synthetic and land field data

title: Adaptive subtraction with 3D U-net and 3D data windows to suppress seismic multiples
authors: Huang, J., Fu, L., Ma, J., Du, X., Li, Z., Sun, K.
org: Guizhou University, China University of Petroleum (East China), Qingdao University, PowerChina Guiyang
abstract: Proposes 3D U-Net with 3D data windows for adaptive multiple subtraction, capturing spatial continuity along gathers; combined with transfer learning, it improves SNR by ~1 dB over 2D U-Net and cuts computation by 70%

title: Internal multiple suppression with convolutional neural network-based transfer learning
authors: Liu, X., Hu, T., Liu, T., Wei, Z., Xiao, Y., Xie, F., Duan, W., Cui, Y., Peng, G.
org: Peking University, SINOPEC, PetroChina Tarim Oilfield
abstract: Proposes a U-Net based CNN with multiple learning and transfer learning for internal multiple suppression, using VE-generated labels and fine-tuning to achieve high efficiency and better primary preservation

title: Deep learning-based closed-loop surface-related multiple Elimination
authors: He, Z., Li, Z., Li, Z., Xu, Y., Tan, G., Yue, Y., Wang, P.
org: China University of Petroleum (East China), Sinopec, CNOOC, Southwest Petroleum University
abstract: Proposes DL-CLSRME: a U-Net model that maps SRME-predicted multiples to CLSRME-accurate multiples, avoiding adaptive subtraction and waveform inversion for fast, accurate surface-related multiple suppression

title: Deep Learning for Efficient Prestack Seismic Internal Multiples Suppression with Global-Local Feature Fusion and Optuna Optimization
authors: Zhang, M., Hu, T., Duan, W., Li, X.
org: Peking University, CNPC, Kunlun Digital Technology
abstract: Proposes GLO-UNet with Global-Local Feature Fusion (Transformer+DenseNet) and Optuna-optimized hybrid loss for efficient prestack internal multiple suppression, outperforming baselines in SNR and primary preservation

title: Deep learning-based high-resolution Radon transform and its application to multiple elimination
authors: Li, Z., Zhang, H., Tan, G., Li, Z., He, Z., Zhang, Q., Wang, P., Dong, L.
org: China University of Petroleum (East China), CNPC, CNOOC, BGP
abstract: Proposes a deep learning-based high-resolution Radon transform using U-Net to map adjoint solutions to high-resolution Radon domain results, replacing iterative inversion for fast, stable multiple elimination with lower training cost and better sparsity

title: Accurate Multiple Wave Suppression Using Data Correlation and Long Short-Term Memory Networks
authors: Geng, H., Peng, S., Cui, X., He, T., Xu, N., Du, W.
org: China University of Mining and Technology (Beijing)
abstract: Proposes DC-LSTM by combining data correlation and LSTM to accurately suppress multiples in CMP gathers while preserving primaries and avoiding pseudo-frequency artifacts

title: Adaptive_Subtraction_of_Post-Stack_Surface_Multiples_Using_the_Pseudo-Seismic-Data-Based_Convolutional_Neural_Network
authors: Liu, L., Hu, T.
abstract: Proposes P-CNN for adaptive subtraction of post-stack surface multiples by introducing Hilbert-transformed pseudo-seismic data as input, achieving better amplitude-phase matching and higher SNR than CNN and L1-norm methods

title: Adaptive_Surface-Related_Multiple_Subtraction_Based_on_Convolutional_Neural_Network
authors: Liu, L., Hu, T.
abstract: Proposes P-CNN for adaptive subtraction of post-stack surface multiples by introducing Hilbert-transformed pseudo-seismic data as input, achieving better amplitude-phase matching and higher SNR than CNN and L1-norm methods

title: Seismic_Internal_Multiple_Suppression_Based_on_Convolutional_Neural_Network
authors: Liu, X., Hu, T., Wang, S., Liu, T., Wei, Z.
org: Peking University, China University of Petroleum (Beijing), SINOPEC
abstract: Proposes a U-shaped CNN for internal multiple suppression using virtual event labels, vertical patch input, and multiple data augmentation, achieving low memory usage, high efficiency, and better primary preservation

title: Seismic_Surface-Related_Multiples_Suppression_Based_on_SAGAN
authors: Tao, L., Ren, H., Ye, Y., Jiang, J.
org: Zhejiang University, PetroChina Hangzhou Research Institute of Geology
abstract: Proposes SAGAN (self-attention GAN with U-Net generator and Markov discriminator) for end-to-end surface-related multiple suppression, achieving high SNR, efficient inference, and robustness to missing near-offset traces

title: Surface-Related_Multiple_Suppression_Based_on_Prestack_Seismic_Data_Regularization_Using_Deep_Neural_Network_Wavefield_Extrapolation
authors: Duan, W., Liu, J., Lei, G., Zheng, D., Chen, F., Sun, H., Hu, T.
org: CNPC, Peking University, Xinjiang Key Laboratory
abstract: Proposes WEDNN for surface-related multiple suppression by fusing t-x and f-k domain features to extrapolate far-offset SRME results to missing near-offset traces, reducing information loss by 48.83% and improving efficiency by 88.3%

title: U-Net-Based_Adaptive_Subtraction_Using_Three_Frequency_Bands_of_Simulated_Multiples_for_Their_Suppression
authors: Ma, J., Sun, K., Dai, X., Li, B., Wang, Y., Li, Z.
org: Qingdao University, PetroChina, BGP Inc., CAS
abstract: Proposes UNetFM using three frequency bands of simulated multiples as three-channel U-Net input for adaptive subtraction, achieving higher SNR and better residual multiple removal than traditional and single-channel U-Net methods

title: Attenuating free-surface multiples and ghost reflection from seismic data using a trace-by-trace convolutional neural network approach
authors: Kiraz, M. S. R., Snieder, R., Sheiman, J.
abstract: Proposes a trace-by-trace 1D CNN to simultaneously attenuate free-surface multiples and remove ghost reflections, no dense acquisition or adaptive subtraction needed, preserves primaries even when overlapped

title: Multiple Elimination Using Model-Driven Self-Supervised Learning With an Attention Mechanism
authors: Shi, Y., Bao, P., Zhang, W.
org: Northeast Petroleum University, Sinopec, University of Alberta
abstract: Proposes model-driven self-supervised learning with attention mechanism + U-Net for multiple elimination; uses conventional SRME-predicted multiples as input, hybrid L1+L2 loss, better preserves primaries and improves efficiency

title: Physics-Guided Deep Learning for Adaptive Surface-Related Multiple Subtraction
authors: Zhang, D., Verschuur, E.
abstract: Proposes a physics-guided deep learning (PGDL) framework with dual-channel U-Net for adaptive surface-related multiple subtraction by learning non-linear residual mapping from full wavefield and physics-based multiple predictions

title: Unsupervised Local Primary-and-Multiple Orthogonalization Learning for Seismic Multiple Leakage Estimation
authors: Zhang, D., Rui, Z.
org: Delft University of Technology, China University of Petroleum (Beijing)
abstract: Proposes unsupervised local primary-and-multiple orthogonalization learning (ULPMOL) with U-Net and local orthogonality loss for seismic multiple leakage estimation without labeled data

title: CNN-based adaptive subtraction for the removal of seismic multiples
authors: Li, Z., Xie, F., Ma, J., Qi, Z., Wang, Y.
abstract: Proposes a deep CNN-based adaptive subtraction method with L1 primary constraint and L2 regularization, using modeled multiples as input and original data as label to learn nonlinear mismatches, outperforming LR and SVR in multiple removal and primary preservation

title: Accelerating the U-Net based adaptive subtraction with transfer learning for removing seismic multiples in field data
authors: Li, Z., Ma, J., Qi, Z., Zhang, L., Xie, F., Yang, J.
abstract: Proposes transfer learning accelerated U-Net for adaptive multiple subtraction, reusing prior parameters to reduce epochs, achieving same accuracy with ~40% less computation time for field data

title: Dual-branch enhanced U-Net algorithm for multi-source separation and multiple suppression in seismic data processing
authors: Zhang, Q., Lv, X., Li, Y., Peng, B., Tang, S.
abstract: Proposes dual-branch enhanced U-Net (MCESA-Unet) with ALC and ESA modules for simultaneous multi-source separation and multiple suppression, outperforming baseline models on synthetic and field data

title: U-Net++ Based Subshallow Gas-Scattered Image Conditioning: Small-Scale Case Study of Seismic Data Acquired in the Korean East Sea
authors: Lee, J., Lee, M. J., Park, H., Jun, H., Cho, Y.
org: Seoul National University, Kyungpook National University
abstract: Proposes self-supervised U-Net++ with attention module for shallow gas–distorted seismic image conditioning; uses hybrid L1+SSIM loss, random tapering/cropping for training; reconstructs missing traces, improves stratigraphic continuity, outperforms U-Net, U-Net++, and conventional Pyseistr interpolation on Korean East Sea data

title: Accelerating seismic scattered noise attenuation in offset-vector tile domain: Application of deep learning
authors: Liu, D., Wang, X., Yang, X., Mao, H., Sacchi, M. D., Chen, W.
org: Xi’an Jiaotong University, Xinjiang Oilfield, University of Alberta
abstract: Proposes OVT-domain + 3D CNN for fast seismic scattered noise attenuation; uses direct signal learning (no residual), 3D CWT for high-quality labels, mid-offset OVT training; achieves 10× speedup vs 3D CWT, better denoising & generalization

title: A deep learning approach to separating scattered direct waves from reflection seismic records on rugged surface: Synthetic data examples
authors: Gong, R., Wang, Y., Geng, J.
abstract: Proposes convolutional autoencoder (CAE) to separate scattered direct waves (SDWs) from rugged-surface seismic records; uses spectral element method to generate paired synthetic data (with/without reflectors) for supervised training; CAE outperforms F–K filtering and CNN in denoising, efficiency, and preserving reflections on single-reflector, four-reflector, and modified Marmousi2 models

title: Enhancing Seismic Data Quality With Gabor‐Kernel Unsupervised Denoising of Near-Surface Scattering Noise
authors: Zhou, Z., Saad, O. M., Liu, S., Liu, Y., Alkhalifah, T.
abstract: Proposes D-GDLNet, an unsupervised denoising method based on Gabor dictionary learning for near-surface scattered noise attenuation; outperforms GDLNet, DRR, and Seislet transform while preserving reflections and steeply dipping signals

title: Seismic ground-roll noise attenuation using deep learning
authors: Kaur, H., Fomel, S., Pham, N.
abstract: Uses CycleGAN for seismic ground-roll attenuation with labels from LTF and RNR, achieving fast and accurate noise suppression

title: Unsupervised dual learning for seismic data denoising in the absence of labelled data
authors: Zhao, Y. X., Li, Y., Wu, N.
abstract: Proposes unsupervised DeGANs based on CycleGAN for seismic denoising without labeled data; trained on unpaired real noisy and synthetic clean data, suppresses random noise and ground roll thoroughly with almost no false signals

title: Ground roll intelligent suppression based on spatial domain synchrosqueezing wavelet transform convolutional neural network
authors: Xing, L., Lin, H., Wang, L., Xu, Z., Liu, H., Li, Q.
abstract: Proposes ST-CNN combining spatial domain synchrosqueezing wavelet transform and CNN for intelligent high-precision ground roll suppression in seismic data

title: A self-supervised scheme for ground roll suppression
authors: Liu, S., Birnie, C., Bakulin, A., Dawood, A., Silvestrov, I., Alkhalifah, T.
org: King Abdullah University of Science and Technology
abstract: Proposes self-supervised blind-fan network with U-Net for aliased ground roll suppression without clean labeled data

title: Slope assisted Physics-informed neural networks for seismic signal separation with applications on ground roll removal and interpolation
authors: Brandolin, F., Ravasi, M., Alkhalifah, T.
org: King Abdullah University of Science and Technology
abstract: Proposes slope-assisted PINNslope with single/double slope constraints and time-derivative loss for seismic signal separation, enabling ground-roll removal, deblending, and aliased data interpolation

title: A Ground-Roll Separation Method Based on Neural Networks With Morphological Similarity Loss
authors: Tian, X., Ao, Y., Li, Y., Lu, W.
abstract: Proposes U-Net with Gabor-based morphological similarity loss (MSLoss) for pseudo-low-frequency signal generation, combined with dictionary learning for ground-roll separation

title: Simultaneous Suppression of Random Noise and Ground Roll With a Fuzzy Logic-Guided Deep Network
authors: Zuo, X., Ma, J., Liao, Z., Chen, X.
abstract: Proposes DASDNet with fuzzy logic encoder, STFT-DWT time-frequency decomposition, and variance-aware attention to simultaneously suppress random noise and ground roll

title: Ground-roll attenuation using dual-model self-supervised selective learning with blind horizontal convolutional neural networks
authors: Son, Y. H., Park, H., Cho, Y., Min, D. J.
abstract: Proposes blind horizontal network (BHN) + dual-model self-supervised selective learning (dSSSL) for ground-roll attenuation, avoiding U-Net identity mapping and improving separation stability

title: Ground-roll attenuation in high-dimensional domain using multi-scale decomposition and attention mechanism network
authors: Yan, T., Dai, Y., Wang, Z.
abstract: Proposes NSST-MSANet: Non-Subsampled Shearlet Transform + multi-scale attention network for ground-roll attenuation in high-dimensional domain, reducing signal-noise overlap and preserving useful signals

title: Attribute-constrained unsupervised ground-roll suppression via mid-band amplitude–phase fidelity and wavefield orthogonalization
authors: Wang, K., Rao, Y., Hu, T., Zhao, Z.
org: China University of Petroleum (Beijing) & Peking University
abstract: Proposes attribute-constrained unsupervised network (UDNN-ACs) for ground-roll suppression, combining mid-band amplitude–phase fidelity and wavefield orthogonalization loss without labeled data
